Title: A bacterial platform for fermentative production of plant alkaloids

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutical benzylisoquinoline alkaloid branch-point intermediate and non-narcotic building block for antimalarial and anticancer drug development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of tyrR derepressed aromatic amino-acid biosynthesis, increased L-tyrosine supply, and enabled higher downstream production of dopamine and (S)-reticuline from simple carbon sources
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tyrR was disrupted to relieve repression of aromatic amino-acid biosynthetic genes. 2. The tyrR-null background was part of the engineered L-tyrosine-overproducing strain producing ~4.37 g l-1 L-tyrosine from glycerol. 3. This precursor-enhanced background increased (S)-reticuline output versus wild-type E. coli carrying the same synthetic pathway.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: simple medium with glucose or glycerol as carbon source; jar fermentation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful chassis edit to derepress aromatic amino-acid flux for microbial alkaloid engineering; can be combined with pathway-gene stacking for other aromatic amino-acid-derived metabolites
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not specifically reported; possible broader aromatic-pathway perturbation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a chassis-enabling core engineering factor in Results, though not a direct alkaloid biosynthetic enzyme
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR of pathway genes; no accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream; committed entry step to shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step supported by engineering logic; feedback-resistant entry enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and erythrose-4-phosphate to form DAHP, initiating the shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression in precursor-overproducing strain; fermentation; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased carbon flux into aromatic amino-acid biosynthesis, supporting high L-tyrosine production and downstream reticuline synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. aroGfbr was overexpressed in the shikimate pathway to increase flux to L-tyrosine. 2. Together with tyrAfbr, ppsA, and tktA, it enabled ~4.37 g l-1 L-tyrosine production from glycerol. 3. This precursor supply supported downstream dopamine and (S)-reticuline biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of host precursor module rather than plant alkaloid-specific module
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core precursor engineering target for microbial production of aromatic amino-acid-derived alkaloids and phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR of downstream pathway genes only; no accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Bifunctional chorismate mutase/prephenate dehydrogenase, feedback-inhibition-resistant variant
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: L-tyrosine biosynthesis branch of aromatic amino-acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key branch control step supported by engineering logic
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to prephenate and oxidatively decarboxylates prephenate toward 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate in tyrosine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression in precursor-overproducing strain; fermentation; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhanced L-tyrosine branch flux and precursor supply for dopamine and reticuline biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tyrAfbr was overexpressed as a feedback-resistant tyrosine-branch enzyme. 2. It contributed to strong L-tyrosine overproduction in engineered E. coli. 3. Elevated precursor supply enabled fermentative (S)-reticuline production from simple carbon sources.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful branch-pathway engineering node for aromatic alkaloid fermentation platforms
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR of downstream pathway genes only; no accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: central carbon metabolism supporting shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: precursor supply to common aromatic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Regenerates phosphoenolpyruvate from pyruvate to increase precursor supply for the shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression in precursor-overproducing strain; fermentation; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improved precursor availability for aromatic amino-acid biosynthesis, supporting reticuline production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ppsA was overexpressed to increase metabolic flow into the shikimate pathway. 2. It formed part of the L-tyrosine-overproducing chassis. 3. This chassis enabled downstream alkaloid production from simple carbon sources.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central metabolic support gene rather than dedicated alkaloid enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for carbon-flux optimization in microbial production of aromatic metabolites

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: pentose phosphate pathway supporting shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: precursor supply to common aromatic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers two-carbon ketol units in the pentose phosphate pathway, increasing erythrose-4-phosphate supply for the shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression in precursor-overproducing strain; fermentation; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhanced E4P supply for L-tyrosine overproduction and downstream reticuline synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tktA was overexpressed to increase metabolic flow into the shikimate pathway. 2. It supported high L-tyrosine production from glycerol. 3. Improved precursor availability contributed to fermentative (S)-reticuline production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful flux-enhancing component for microbial aromatic secondary metabolite production

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates L-tyrosine to L-DOPA; also has o-diphenolase side activity oxidizing L-DOPA/dopamine to quinones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; metabolite profiling; comparison of TYR orthologs; fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled conversion of L-tyrosine to L-DOPA and supported downstream dopamine and reticuline production, but lower yield than RsTYR due to side oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among tested TYRs, only Streptomyces castaneoglobisporus TYR converted L-tyrosine to L-DOPA in E. coli. 2. With DODC, it enabled dopamine production (~260 mg l-1) and reticuline production (~6.24 mg l-1 in glycerol medium). 3. Its o-diphenolase activity likely destabilized intermediates and limited reticuline yield relative to RsTYR.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: requires copper; glucose or glycerol fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Forms melanin-like pigment and may oxidize L-DOPA/dopamine, reducing pathway efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected because E. coli lacks BH4 and cannot use canonical tyrosine hydroxylase efficiently
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in engineered E. coli; set as reference in qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates utility of non-native hydroxylase substitutes, but lower-o-diphenolase variants are preferable for industrial alkaloid production
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR of pathway genes; no accession

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: accessory factor for upstream hydroxylation step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adaptor protein required for functional ScTYR activity in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression with ScTYR; metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports ScTYR-dependent L-DOPA formation and downstream alkaloid production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ORF378 was co-expressed with ScTYR as an adaptor protein. 2. The selected Streptomyces TYR system required only copper and ORF378 for activity in E. coli. 3. It supported L-DOPA/dopamine/(S)-reticuline production in the ScTYR-based platform.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Accessory rather than catalytic enzyme, but experimentally part of the core engineered module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected by qRT-PCR in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Needed when deploying ScTYR-based hydroxylation modules
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR of pathway genes; no accession

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key improvement point in optimized pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates L-tyrosine to L-DOPA with lower o-diphenolase side activity than ScTYR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly increased dopamine (~1.05 g l-1) and reticuline production (up to 46 mg l-1) compared with ScTYR-based platform
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsTYR was substituted for ScTYR because of its lower o-diphenolase activity. 2. The RsTYR platform produced ~7-fold more reticuline and ~4-fold more dopamine than the ScTYR platform. 3. Reduced side oxidation of intermediates was inferred to improve pathway efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not specifically reported; tyrosinases may still generate some quinone side products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This ortholog optimization was central to achieving the highest reported reticuline yield
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred tyrosinase choice for microbial BIA engineering due to higher product yield and reduced intermediate loss

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates L-DOPA to dopamine with >10^3-fold preference for L-DOPA over other aromatic amino acids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; metabolite profiling; enzyme selectivity rationale; fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Efficiently converted L-DOPA to dopamine, reduced accumulation of L-DOPA and melanin-like pigment, and supported reticuline biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A Pseudomonas putida DOPA-specific decarboxylase was selected to avoid tyramine formation from L-tyrosine. 2. Co-expression with TYR yielded ~260 mg l-1 dopamine with ScTYR and higher dopamine with RsTYR. 3. High affinity for L-DOPA likely helped outcompete TYR side oxidation and improved flux to reticuline.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene expression level exceeded TYR in qRT-PCR, consistent with rapid L-DOPA conversion
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High relative transcript level in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-specificity decarboxylase is important to suppress by-product entry and preserve product stereochemical purity in engineered alkaloid pathways
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR of pathway genes; no accession

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: dopamine oxidation to aldehyde coupling substrate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively deaminates dopamine to 3,4-dihydroxyphenylacetaldehyde used for norcoclaurine/norlaudanosoline formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression of codon-optimized gene; fermentation; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled synthetic conversion from dopamine to BIA skeleton in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Microbial MAO was used to create the aldehyde partner needed for condensation to the BIA skeleton. 2. A codon-optimized MAO was incorporated into the optimized pET-21d pathway cassette. 3. This artificial step helped circumvent the incomplete and complex native plant pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: If non-specific amines formed, MAO could generate unwanted aldehydes and by-products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selection of DODC specificity was important to prevent MAO use of tyramine-derived side products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in engineered E. coli by qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful non-plant bypass enzyme for simplifying microbial reconstruction of BIA pathways
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR of pathway genes; no accession

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes stereoselective Pictet-Spengler condensation of dopamine with aldehyde substrate to form the (S)-configured BIA scaffold
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for direct scaffold formation toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; omission analysis; chiral LC-MS; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Conferred exclusive (S)-reticuline production in the new platform; without NCS, (R)-reticuline was not detected under fermentative conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCS was incorporated to generate the stereoselective BIA scaffold. 2. The newly constructed system produced exclusively (S)-reticuline. 3. NCS absence altered stereochemical outcome logic, confirming its role in directing (S)-configured product formation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Plant gene expression was lower than microbial genes in qRT-PCR, possibly limiting pathway efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper discusses norcoclaurine/norlaudanosoline terminology in synthetic scheme; NCS was essential for stereoselective pathway behavior
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low relative transcript versus microbial genes in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical enzyme for optical purity of microbial BIA products
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR of pathway genes; no accession

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: methylation steps from BIA scaffold to reticuline
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to the 6-hydroxyl of the BIA intermediate en route to reticuline
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for downstream conversion to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; pathway reconstruction; chiral product analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Participated in downstream methylation sequence producing reticuline; likely favored (S)- over (R)-substrates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 6OMT was one of the three methyltransferases needed to convert the BIA scaffold to reticuline. 2. Plant methyltransferase transcripts were lower than microbial gene transcripts, suggesting expression may limit flux. 3. Methyltransferases likely react more efficiently with the (S)-form, contributing to exclusive (S)-reticuline accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low expression may constrain conversion efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inserted in pACYC184 together with 4′OMT and CNMT
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower transcript abundance than microbial genes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Expression optimization of plant methyltransferases is a key route to improve microbial reticuline titers
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR of pathway genes; no accession

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: methylation steps from BIA scaffold to reticuline
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to the amine of the BIA intermediate during conversion toward reticuline
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for downstream conversion to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; pathway reconstruction; chiral product analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required as part of methyltransferase module for reticuline formation; likely contributes to preference for (S)-configured intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CNMT was co-expressed with 6OMT and 4′OMT to produce reticuline from the BIA scaffold. 2. Plant methyltransferase transcripts were relatively low in E. coli. 3. Stereoselective processing by methyltransferases was proposed to underlie (S)-reticuline accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low transcript abundance may limit flux
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 4′OMT cannot use (S)-N-methylcoclaurine directly in the absence of CYP80B in the native route; pathway was redesigned to circumvent this issue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower transcript abundance than microbial genes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Optimization of expression/codon usage may increase terminal alkaloid yields
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR of pathway genes; no accession

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 4′-O-methylation of a hydroxylated N-methylated BIA intermediate on the route to reticuline
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product or immediate last precursor in redesigned pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; pathway reconstruction; qRT-PCR; chiral product analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Participated in final methylation module leading to reticuline accumulation in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4′OMT was included with 6OMT and CNMT in the reticuline-producing module. 2. The native plant pathway was bypassed because 4′OMT alone cannot catalyze (S)-N-methylcoclaurine without CYP80B-mediated hydroxylation in the canonical route. 3. In the redesigned microbial pathway, methyltransferase activity contributed to (S)-reticuline accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low plant-gene expression may reduce conversion efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes lack of CYP80B in the system and redesign of flux to circumvent problematic CYP step
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower transcript abundance than microbial genes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Illustrates need to redesign plant pathways around difficult CYP steps for microbial production
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR of pathway genes; no accession
